message:
Last week my phone died right as I was testing our smoke alarms—and I realized I had no idea where I'd written down our family's meet-up spot. My kids looked at me like I'd forgotten how to adult. 😅

Turns out, relying on my phone for *everything* (including my own emergency plan) was a pretty glaring gap. So I did something old-school: wrote it down on actual paper and taped it inside our kitchen cabinet. Now everyone knows the plan, with or without a charged battery.

It got me thinking about all the little tech dependencies we have around home safety—from how we'd contact each other to remembering which bedroom window is easiest to exit. Worth a quick think-through, especially if you've got kids who only know how to text.

Do you keep your family emergency plan somewhere that doesn't need wifi or a battery?
